{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Company Secretary",
  "description": "Qualis Business Support Services Limited - Our client is a Pan-African trade infrastructure and venture studio company that incubates agribusiness MSMEs across West Africa.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Company Secretary

Job Location: Central Business District, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The role ensures the Board, management, and operations adhere to applicable Nigerian and ECOWAS regulatory frameworks, &amp; that all statutory obligations are met with precision.


Requirements


	Degree in Law &amp; Associate or Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Secretaries and Administrators of Nigeria (ICSAN) &mdash; ACIS or FCIS.
	Minimum 3 years of post-qualification experience in a company secretarial or corporate governance role.
	Deep working knowledge of CAMA 2020, CAC filing requirements, and Nigerian corporate law.
	Proven experience handling KYC and corporate documentation for banking and institutional engagements.
	Demonstrated ability to support Board operations across a fast-paced, multi-entity business environment.
	Strong drafting skills for Board papers, resolutions, agreements, and regulatory correspondence.


Core Competencies:


	Governance Integrity
	Regulatory Intelligence
	Stakeholder Management
	Precision &amp; Attention to Detail
	Discretion &amp; Confidentiality
	Cross-Border Competence.


Salary
N600,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: adanne.osuagwu@gmail.com using &quot;Company Secretary&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Company Secretary Job at Qualis Business Support Services Limited




        
    

        
        Qualis Business Support Services Limited - Our client is a Pan-African trade infrastructure and venture studio company that incubates agribusiness MSMEs across West Africa.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Company Secretary

Job Location: Central Business District, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The role ensures the Board, management, and operations adhere to applicable Nigerian and ECOWAS regulatory frameworks, & that all statutory obligations are met with precision.


Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Degree in Law & Associate or Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Secretaries and Administrators of Nigeria (ICSAN) — ACIS or FCIS.
	Minimum 3 years of post-qualification experience in a company secretarial or corporate governance role.
	Deep working knowledge of CAMA 2020, CAC filing requirements, and Nigerian corporate law.
	Proven experience handling KYC and corporate documentation for banking and institutional engagements.
	Demonstrated ability to support Board operations across a fast-paced, multi-entity business environment.
	Strong drafting skills for Board papers, resolutions, agreements, and regulatory correspondence.


Core Competencies:


	Governance Integrity
	Regulatory Intelligence
	Stakeholder Management
	Precision & Attention to Detail
	Discretion & Confidentiality
	Cross-Border Competence.


Salary
N600,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using "Company Secretary" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Internal Audit",
  "description": "Renmoney - We are a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria. We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Internal Audit

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Who We Are


	We&#39;re a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria.
	We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experience.


The Role


	The Head of Internal Audit will provide independent, objective assurance and advisory services designed to improve the Bank&#39;s operations and strengthen its internal control environment.
	The role is responsible for leading the Internal Audit function, evaluating risk management processes, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, and assessing the effectiveness of governance, operational, financial, and technology controls across the organization.
	The successful candidate will play a strategic role in safeguarding the Bank&#39;s assets, improving operational efficiency, supporting regulatory compliance, and promoting a strong culture of accountability and risk awareness.


What You Will Do
Internal Audit Strategy &amp; Leadershi:


	Develop and implement the Bank&#39;s annual risk-based internal audit plan
	Provide strategic leadership and direction for the Internal Audit function
	Ensure audit activities are aligned with regulatory requirements, business objectives, and emerging risks
	Present audit findings, reports, and recommendations to Executive Management and the Board Audit Committee
	Continuously improve audit methodologies, frameworks, and reporting standards


Risk Management &amp; Internal Control:


	Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls across business operations, financial reporting, technology, and compliance processes
	Assess risk management frameworks and recommend improvements where necessary
	Monitor control gaps, emerging risks, and remediation activities across the Bank
	Conduct periodic reviews of operational, financial, regulatory, and IT risks


Audit Execution &amp; Assuranc:


	Oversee the execution of operational, financial, compliance, and IT audits across the organization
	Ensure audit assignments are completed efficiently, accurately, and within agreed timelines
	Review audit findings, working papers, and recommendations to ensure quality and consistency
	Conduct investigations into suspected fraud, misconduct, or control breaches where required
	Ensure proper documentation and maintenance of audit records and reports.


Regulatory Compliance &amp; Governance:


	Ensure compliance with CBN regulations, internal policies, and applicable regulatory requirements
	Liaise with regulators, external auditors, and relevant stakeholders on audit-related matters
	Monitor compliance with corporate governance standards and industry best practices
	Support regulatory examinations and external audit engagements.&nbsp;


Stakeholder Engagement &amp; Advisory:


	Partner with business leaders to provide advisory support on process improvement and risk mitigation initiatives
	Promote a strong culture of internal control, ethics, and compliance across the organization
	Provide guidance to management on governance, operational efficiency, and control best practices
	Build strong working relationships across departments while maintaining audit independence.&nbsp;


Reporting &amp; Analytics:


	Prepare periodic audit reports and dashboards for Executive Management and the Board Audit Committee
	Track audit findings, management responses, and remediation status
	Analyze audit trends and provide insights to support decision-making and continuous improvement.&nbsp;


Requirements What You Bring
Education &amp; Professional Qualifications:


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or related discipline
	Professional certifications such as ACA, ACCA, CIA, CISA, or related certifications are required
	Master&#39;s degree or additional professional qualifications are an added advantage


Experience:


	Minimum of 8-12 years of relevant experience in Internal Audit, Risk Management, Compliance, or Financial Control within banking or financial services
	Minimum of 4 years in a leadership or managerial role
	Strong understanding of banking operations, regulatory requirements, risk management, and internal control frameworks
	Experience leading operational, financial, compliance, and IT audits.


Technical Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of audit methodologies, risk assessment, and internal control frameworks
	Good understanding of CBN regulations, IFRS, and corporate governance principles
	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills
	Experience with audit management systems, ERP platforms, and data analytics tools
	Excellent report writing, presentation, and stakeholder management skills
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and professional judgement.


This Role Is Ideal For You If:


	You are highly analytical and detail-oriented
	You can balance governance responsibilities with practical business realities
	You are confident engaging with senior stakeholders and regulators
	You are passionate about strengthening controls, improving processes, and managing risk
	You thrive in dynamic, fast-paced environments and can lead with objectivity and professionalism.


Benefits


	You&rsquo;ll receive competitive compensation and work with amazing people.
	You&rsquo;ll work in a beautiful environment with a flat structure and solve complex, real-world challenges.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-29",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-30",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Head of Internal Audit Job at Renmoney




        
    

        
        Renmoney - We are a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria. We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Internal Audit

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Who We Are
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We're a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria.
	We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experience.


The Role


	The Head of Internal Audit will provide independent, objective assurance and advisory services designed to improve the Bank's operations and strengthen its internal control environment.
	The role is responsible for leading the Internal Audit function, evaluating risk management processes, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, and assessing the effectiveness of governance, operational, financial, and technology controls across the organization.
	The successful candidate will play a strategic role in safeguarding the Bank's assets, improving operational efficiency, supporting regulatory compliance, and promoting a strong culture of accountability and risk awareness.


What You Will Do
Internal Audit Strategy & Leadershi:


	Develop and implement the Bank's annual risk-based internal audit plan
	Provide strategic leadership and direction for the Internal Audit function
	Ensure audit activities are aligned with regulatory requirements, business objectives, and emerging risks
	Present audit findings, reports, and recommendations to Executive Management and the Board Audit Committee
	Continuously improve audit methodologies, frameworks, and reporting standards


Risk Management & Internal Control:


	Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of internal controls across business operations, financial reporting, technology, and compliance processes
	Assess risk management frameworks and recommend improvements where necessary
	Monitor control gaps, emerging risks, and remediation activities across the Bank
	Conduct periodic reviews of operational, financial, regulatory, and IT risks


Audit Execution & Assuranc:


	Oversee the execution of operational, financial, compliance, and IT audits across the organization
	Ensure audit assignments are completed efficiently, accurately, and within agreed timelines
	Review audit findings, working papers, and recommendations to ensure quality and consistency
	Conduct investigations into suspected fraud, misconduct, or control breaches where required
	Ensure proper documentation and maintenance of audit records and reports.


Regulatory Compliance & Governance:


	Ensure compliance with CBN regulations, internal policies, and applicable regulatory requirements
	Liaise with regulators, external auditors, and relevant stakeholders on audit-related matters
	Monitor compliance with corporate governance standards and industry best practices
	Support regulatory examinations and external audit engagements. 


Stakeholder Engagement & Advisory:


	Partner with business leaders to provide advisory support on process improvement and risk mitigation initiatives
	Promote a strong culture of internal control, ethics, and compliance across the organization
	Provide guidance to management on governance, operational efficiency, and control best practices
	Build strong working relationships across departments while maintaining audit independence. 


Reporting & Analytics:


	Prepare periodic audit reports and dashboards for Executive Management and the Board Audit Committee
	Track audit findings, management responses, and remediation status
	Analyze audit trends and provide insights to support decision-making and continuous improvement. 


Requirements What You Bring
Education & Professional Qualifications:


	Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or related discipline
	Professional certifications such as ACA, ACCA, CIA, CISA, or related certifications are required
	Master's degree or additional professional qualifications are an added advantage


Experience:


	Minimum of 8-12 years of relevant experience in Internal Audit, Risk Management, Compliance, or Financial Control within banking or financial services
	Minimum of 4 years in a leadership or managerial role
	Strong understanding of banking operations, regulatory requirements, risk management, and internal control frameworks
	Experience leading operational, financial, compliance, and IT audits.


Technical Skills & Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of audit methodologies, risk assessment, and internal control frameworks
	Good understanding of CBN regulations, IFRS, and corporate governance principles
	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills
	Experience with audit management systems, ERP platforms, and data analytics tools
	Excellent report writing, presentation, and stakeholder management skills
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and professional judgement.


This Role Is Ideal For You If:


	You are highly analytical and detail-oriented
	You can balance governance responsibilities with practical business realities
	You are confident engaging with senior stakeholders and regulators
	You are passionate about strengthening controls, improving processes, and managing risk
	You thrive in dynamic, fast-paced environments and can lead with objectivity and professionalism.


Benefits


	You’ll receive competitive compensation and work with amazing people.
	You’ll work in a beautiful environment with a flat structure and solve complex, real-world challenges.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Outlet Manager",
  "description": "Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Outlet Manager

Job Location: Taraba
Employment Type: Ful-time&nbsp;

Job Summary


	The Outlet Manager will oversee the daily outlet operations, driving sales performance, ensuring excellent customer service, and enforcing company policies and procedures.
	The role requires strong leadership, operational efficiency, and the ability to contribute to business growth while maintaining Mshel Homes Limited&rsquo;s standards and brand reputation.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and supervise all daily activities of the Taraba outlet.
	Drive sales growth and ensure achievement of monthly and quarterly targets.
	Lead, train, and monitor the performance of Sales Executives and Customer Care staff.
	Develop and implement effective sales and marketing strategies.
	Ensure excellent customer experience and prompt resolution of client inquiries.
	Monitor market trends and provide feedback to management.
	Maintain proper documentation of sales, client records, and transactions.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, procedures, and operational standards.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and key stakeholders.
	Prepare and submit periodic performance and sales reports to management.
	Oversee inventory and ensure proper management of all company assets within the outlet.&nbsp;
	Coordinate promotional activities, campaigns, and site inspections to boost sales conversion.
	Ensure timely follow-up on leads and conversion of prospects into clients.
	Manage outlet budgets, expenses, and cost control measures effectively.
	Identify business opportunities within the region and recommend expansion strategies to management.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Marketing, Real Estate, or a related field.
	5-7 years of experience in sales, operations, or branch management, preferably in real estate or related industries.
	Prior supervisory or leadership experience is an added advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong leadership and people-management skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
	Sound knowledge of sales operations and customer relationship management.
	Good analytical, reporting, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet targets.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM tools.


Personal Attributes:


	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Result-oriented and proactive.
	Strong organizational and time-management skills.
	Customer-focused mindset.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	A collaborative and innovative work environment.
	The chance to make a meaningful impact within the real estate industry.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: recruitment@mshelhomes.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Outlet Manager Job at Mshel Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Outlet Manager

Job Location: Taraba
Employment Type: Ful-time 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	The Outlet Manager will oversee the daily outlet operations, driving sales performance, ensuring excellent customer service, and enforcing company policies and procedures.
	The role requires strong leadership, operational efficiency, and the ability to contribute to business growth while maintaining Mshel Homes Limited’s standards and brand reputation.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and supervise all daily activities of the Taraba outlet.
	Drive sales growth and ensure achievement of monthly and quarterly targets.
	Lead, train, and monitor the performance of Sales Executives and Customer Care staff.
	Develop and implement effective sales and marketing strategies.
	Ensure excellent customer experience and prompt resolution of client inquiries.
	Monitor market trends and provide feedback to management.
	Maintain proper documentation of sales, client records, and transactions.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, procedures, and operational standards.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and key stakeholders.
	Prepare and submit periodic performance and sales reports to management.
	Oversee inventory and ensure proper management of all company assets within the outlet. 
	Coordinate promotional activities, campaigns, and site inspections to boost sales conversion.
	Ensure timely follow-up on leads and conversion of prospects into clients.
	Manage outlet budgets, expenses, and cost control measures effectively.
	Identify business opportunities within the region and recommend expansion strategies to management.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Marketing, Real Estate, or a related field.
	5-7 years of experience in sales, operations, or branch management, preferably in real estate or related industries.
	Prior supervisory or leadership experience is an added advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong leadership and people-management skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
	Sound knowledge of sales operations and customer relationship management.
	Good analytical, reporting, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet targets.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM tools.


Personal Attributes:


	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Result-oriented and proactive.
	Strong organizational and time-management skills.
	Customer-focused mindset.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	A collaborative and innovative work environment.
	The chance to make a meaningful impact within the real estate industry.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mechanical Structural Project Supervisor",
  "description": "Beta Glass, a proud member of the Frigoglass Group, stands as a strategic partner to international beverage bottlers. Positioned as a major supplier of glass packaging, we play a crucial role in the burgeoning markets of West and Central Africa.


Our diverse range of glass containers offers superior packaging solutions to clients across various sectors, including soft drinks, beer, spirits, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. We are committed to delivering excellence in our products. At Beta Glass, we are not just suppliers; we are partners in progress, dedicated to setting new standards in the glass packaging industry.



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Structural Project Supervisor

Job Location: Delta



Job Purpose


	The role oversees project planning, designing, execution, supervision and documentation alongside plant utility installations and maintenance for optimal performance.


Responsibilities


	Oversee all civil, structural and mechanical projects to achieve quality
	Conduct detailed structural analysis and simulations to ensure the integrity and reliability of designs.
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g., engineering, production, quality control) to ensure seamless integration of components.
	Coordinate with suppliers and vendors to ensure timely delivery of materials and components.
	Monitor project progress and adjust plans as needed to meet objectives.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date engineering documentation, including design specifications, calculations, and drawings.
	Identify and mitigate potential resource constraints to ensure project continuity.
	Ensure safe work practice on every project activity initiated by the Project session.
	Implement all requirements of Quality Management Systems (QMS), Occupational Health and Safety Assessment Series (OHSAS), Environmental Management Systems (EMS) and any other relevant management systems.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mechanical Engineering or any other related fields
	4- 6 years of experience in a similar role
	Knowledge of AutoCAD and other design related software
	Versatile in Primavera, MS Project
	Proficient in equipment installation and maintenance
	Use of Microsoft offices - Excel, Words and PowerPoint
	Good communication skills - oral and written
	Time management and report writing skills
	Problem solving and strategic thinking skills.

Mechanical Structural Project Supervisor Job at Beta Glass




        
    

        
        Beta Glass, a proud member of the Frigoglass Group, stands as a strategic partner to international beverage bottlers. Positioned as a major supplier of glass packaging, we play a crucial role in the burgeoning markets of West and Central Africa.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




Our diverse range of glass containers offers superior packaging solutions to clients across various sectors, including soft drinks, beer, spirits, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. We are committed to delivering excellence in our products. At Beta Glass, we are not just suppliers; we are partners in progress, dedicated to setting new standards in the glass packaging industry.



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Structural Project Supervisor

Job Location: Delta



Job Purpose


	The role oversees project planning, designing, execution, supervision and documentation alongside plant utility installations and maintenance for optimal performance.


Responsibilities


	Oversee all civil, structural and mechanical projects to achieve quality
	Conduct detailed structural analysis and simulations to ensure the integrity and reliability of designs.
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g., engineering, production, quality control) to ensure seamless integration of components.
	Coordinate with suppliers and vendors to ensure timely delivery of materials and components.
	Monitor project progress and adjust plans as needed to meet objectives.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date engineering documentation, including design specifications, calculations, and drawings.
	Identify and mitigate potential resource constraints to ensure project continuity.
	Ensure safe work practice on every project activity initiated by the Project session.
	Implement all requirements of Quality Management Systems (QMS), Occupational Health and Safety Assessment Series (OHSAS), Environmental Management Systems (EMS) and any other relevant management systems.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mechanical Engineering or any other related fields
	4- 6 years of experience in a similar role
	Knowledge of AutoCAD and other design related software
	Versatile in Primavera, MS Project
	Proficient in equipment installation and maintenance
	Use of Microsoft offices - Excel, Words and PowerPoint
	Good communication skills - oral and written
	Time management and report writing skills
	Problem solving and strategic thinking skills.



     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Client Service Officer",
  "description": "Prestigious Consulting is a professional HR consultancy firm. We focus on improving organizational performance through strategic people &amp; process management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Client Service Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking smart, confident, and customer-focused Client Service Officers for our Lekki showroom. The ideal candidates will be responsible for attending to customers, promoting construction materials, driving sales, and ensuring excellent customer service.


Responsibilities


	Attend to walk-in customers professionally
	Promote and recommend construction materials and related products
	Provide customers with accurate product information and recommendations
	Achieve daily and monthly sales targets
	Maintain proper product display and showroom cleanliness
	Prepare quotations and follow up on customer inquiries
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships
	Handle customer complaints and escalate when necessary
	Support inventory monitoring and showroom operations


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a minimum of OND qualification
	Previous experience in customer service, sales, or retail is an added advantage
	Knowledge of construction or building materials is an advantage
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong customer service and negotiation skills
	Professional appearance and positive attitude
	Must reside in Lekki or nearby areas


Salary
N150,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@prestigiousconsultinggroup.ng using &quot;Client Service Officer &ndash; Lekki&quot; as the subject of ther email.",

Client Service Officer Job at Prestigious Consulting




        
    

        
        Prestigious Consulting is a professional HR consultancy firm. We focus on improving organizational performance through strategic people & process management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Client Service Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking smart, confident, and customer-focused Client Service Officers for our Lekki showroom. The ideal candidates will be responsible for attending to customers, promoting construction materials, driving sales, and ensuring excellent customer service.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Attend to walk-in customers professionally
	Promote and recommend construction materials and related products
	Provide customers with accurate product information and recommendations
	Achieve daily and monthly sales targets
	Maintain proper product display and showroom cleanliness
	Prepare quotations and follow up on customer inquiries
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships
	Handle customer complaints and escalate when necessary
	Support inventory monitoring and showroom operations


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a minimum of OND qualification
	Previous experience in customer service, sales, or retail is an added advantage
	Knowledge of construction or building materials is an advantage
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong customer service and negotiation skills
	Professional appearance and positive attitude
	Must reside in Lekki or nearby areas


Salary
N150,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Client Service Officer – Lekki" as the subject of ther email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
